methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Ascend Elements
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Growth), post-money $3B+

data.narrative:
CORE POWER is a Growth-stage climate and energy company that has raised $100M+ to date. The sector peer universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though narrow-filter peer matching—controlling for stage, valuation tier, and business model—yielded insufficient datapoints for robust median-based valuation.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, anchors valuation to the $100M total-raised proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement, implying $120M. This method is defensible given the company's capital intensity and stage transition dynamics, though it relies on the assumption that total raised correlates meaningfully with post-investment enterprise value. Peer-median triangulation was not applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds; the broader 21-company Climate & Energy cohort lacks sufficient homogeneity to serve as a reliable anchor without introducing material bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a central estimate of $120M. This range reflects methodological constraints rather than precision: the low end represents conservative hold-value, while the high end incorporates modest upside from stage-progression dynamics. Key uncertainties include the true correlation between capital deployed and equity value, absence of revenue or margin data, and limited visibility into competitive positioning within the sector's sub-segments. Further narrowing would require access to comparable transaction multiples or detailed financial performance metrics.
